# Makerchip VS Code Extension Resources

This directory contains reference documentation and examples for TL-Verilog development.
Managed by the Makerchip VS Code extension.

**⚠️ Do not edit files in this directory** - changes will be overwritten on update.

## Contents

This folder contains 9 repositories with TL-Verilog documentation, specifications, examples, libraries, and courses:

- **Makerchip-public** - Core documentation and tutorial examples
- **makerchip_examples** - Wide range of example circuits  
- **LF-Building-a-RISC-V-CPU-Core-Course** - Complete RISC-V course
- **warp-v** - Advanced configurable RISC-V CPU generator
- **warp-v_includes** - RISC-V library definitions
- **tlv_lib** - General-purpose TL-Verilog libraries
- **tlv_flow_lib** - Transaction flow component library
- **M5** - M5 macro processor source code
- **.vscode/skills/tlv-ecosystem.md** - GitHub Copilot skill with comprehensive guide

## GitHub Copilot Integration

**For complete information about TL-Verilog, Makerchip, M5, and Visual Debug**, see:

📘 **[.vscode/skills/tlv-ecosystem.md](.vscode/skills/tlv-ecosystem.md)**

This skill provides Copilot with:
- Technology overviews and how they relate
- References to all specifications and documentation  
- Pointers to examples and tutorials
- Syntax quick reference
- Best practices for assistance

When this folder is in your VS Code workspace, Copilot automatically uses this skill to provide context-aware help for TL-Verilog development.

## Key Documentation Files

- `Makerchip-public/docs/TLXSpec.pdf` - TL-X language specification  
- `Makerchip-public/docs/M5_spec.pdf` - M5 macro processor spec
- `Makerchip-public/docs/VisualDebugUsersGuide.pdf` - Visual Debug guide
- `Makerchip-public/docs/TLV_Macros_Guide.pdf` - TLV macros reference

## Usage

**Add to Workspace**: Right-click this folder in VS Code and select "Add Folder to Workspace"

This enables:
- Quick access to specifications and examples
- GitHub Copilot context from `.vscode/skills/tlv-ecosystem.md`
- Browsing tutorial code and reference designs

**Update Resources**: Run "Makerchip: Update Resources" command in VS Code

## Management

- **Auto-updated**: Refreshed periodically by the extension
- **Safe to browse**: Explore and open files for reference
- **Don't modify**: Local changes will be lost on update  
- **Safe to delete**: Extension will recreate if needed

---
